preamble: Similar to the shift to [e], the raising of [æ] to the open-mid front vowel [ε] is a core component of short front vowel shifts. In some dialects, the entire short vowel system rotates, pushing the 'a' sound up into the 'e' space.

examples.note: The TRAP vowel raises to occupy the DRESS vowel space as part of the NZ Short Front Vowel Shift.
TRAP-DRESS merger context; a push-chain begins with TRAP raising.
Parallel raising of the short front vowel, also documented in Southern Hemisphere Englishes.

sources: Trudgill, P., Gordon, E., & Lewis, G. (1998). New dialect formation and Southern Hemisphere English: The New Zealand short front vowels. Journal of Sociolinguistics, 2, 35–51. https://www.academia.edu/25185544/New_dialect_formation_and_Southern_Hemisphere_English_The_New_Zealand_short_front_vowels
